surges.name: Abrasion
surges.summary: Alters friction on surfaces. Base surge: spend 1+ Investiture (touch, free hand) to infuse an object or surface within reach — up to your surge size, not characters or Invested/infused objects — making it nearly frictionless (1 Investiture/round). Or infuse yourself (any size) to gain Skate (spend 2 focus to Move as a free action in a straight line) and Slide (count one size smaller when squeezing; advantage to escape friction/pressure restraints). Others interacting with a slicked surface must win Agility vs. your Abrasion or fall Prone / drop the object; unwilling characters' gear requires an Abrasion test vs. their Physical defense.

talents.name: Frictionless Motion
talents.summary: While infused with Abrasion, your movement rate increases by 10 feet and you ignore Slowed from difficult terrain, climbing, crawling, and swimming.

talents.name: Graceful Skating
talents.summary: When you Skate, you aren't restricted to moving in a straight line.

talents.name: Slippery Target
talents.summary: While infused with Abrasion, attacks can't graze you and Reactive Strikes against you gain a disadvantage.

talents.name: Slick Combatant
talents.summary: While infused with Abrasion, you may interrupt a Move to use other actions, then finish the remaining movement. Once per round, a melee weapon attack made mid-Move rolls an extra damage die (d4, growing with your Abrasion ranks); if you do, the GM may spend a Complication to knock you Prone and end the Move.

talents.name: Reverse Abrasion
talents.summary: Your Abrasion infusions can increase friction instead: characters gain an advantage on Agility and Athletics tests to interact with the target and ignore Slowed the surface would impose.

talents.name: Stormlight Reclamation
talents.summary: After your infusions expend Investiture at the start of your turn, end any number of infusions within reach and recover the Investiture remaining in them.

talents.name: Distant Surgebinding
talents.summary: Use your surges and their talents as though your reach were 20 feet, without needing to touch the target.

talents.name: Smooth Operator
talents.summary: While you have at least 1 Investiture, you gain the benefits of being infused with Abrasion without spending Investiture, and Skate costs 1 less focus.

surges.name: Adhesion
surges.summary: Binds things together via Full Lashings. Base surge: spend 1+ Investiture (touch, free hand) to stick two unsecured objects within reach and within 5 feet of each other (each up to your surge size; one may instead be a floor/wall/ceiling of any size). Costs 1 Investiture/round. Can't target characters or Invested/infused objects; unwilling characters' gear requires an Adhesion test vs. their Physical defense. Breaking a Lashing takes an Athletics test opposed by your Adhesion. (Bondsmiths also wield this surge, but aren't playable.)

talents.name: Binding Strike
talents.summary: After you hit with a melee attack, spend 1 action or 2 focus to use Adhesion without an action: one target must be an object the struck character holds or wears (the infusion test auto-succeeds), and the paired object/surface within 5 feet needs no touch or free hand.

talents.name: Binding Shot
talents.summary: Binding Strike also works when you hit with a ranged attack at any distance, without touching the targets or having a hand free.

talents.name: Adhesive Trap
talents.summary: Infuse just a portion of one surface (up to your surge size). Any character touching that area — on infusion, while moving through it, or when forced into it — is automatically Fully Lashed to the surface for the infusion's duration.

talents.name: Stormlight Reclamation
talents.summary: After your infusions expend Investiture at the start of your turn, end any number of infusions within reach and recover the Investiture remaining in them.

talents.name: Distant Surgebinding
talents.summary: Use your surges and their talents as though your reach were 20 feet, without touching the target (adhered objects must still be within 5 feet of each other).

talents.name: Extended Adhesion
talents.summary: Full Lashings expend 1 Investiture per number of rounds equal to your Adhesion ranks, instead of every round.

talents.name: Living Adhesion
talents.summary: You may target characters directly with Adhesion (tested like targeting their possessions). A character Lashed to something larger than them is Restrained, takes a disadvantage on physical tests, and attacks against them gain an advantage.

talents.name: Superior Bond
talents.summary: When a character with Strength no higher than your Adhesion ranks tries to break one of your Full Lashings, you win the opposed test automatically.

surges.name: Cohesion
surges.summary: Stoneshaping — softens matter at the axi level. Base surge: infuse 1+ Investiture (touch, free hand) into unattended stone within reach (up to your surge size) to make it moldable like clay (1 Investiture/round); rough shaping is immediate, intricate work takes Interact actions or Cohesion tests. Can't target characters or Invested/infused objects; surfaces bearing an unwilling character require a Cohesion test vs. their Physical defense. When the infusion ends the stone resolidifies — anything caught inside is Immobilized (possibly Restrained) until freed via Athletics opposed by your Cohesion.

talents.name: Stone Spear
talents.summary: Spend 1 Investiture: ranged Cohesion attack vs. Physical defense of a target within 60 feet, rolling 2d4 impact damage.

talents.name: Sinkhole
talents.summary: Spend 1 Investiture; choose a stone-ground area up to your surge size and characters touching it. One Cohesion test vs. each target's Cognitive defense; each success sinks that target into the ground, Immobilized.

talents.name: Tunneling
talents.summary: Spend 1 Investiture to infuse yourself: move through stone as if through difficult terrain, leaving a 5-foot tunnel others can follow; maintain for 1 Investiture at the start of each turn.

talents.name: Memories of Stone
talents.summary: Spend 1 Investiture to commune for 1 round with stone you're touching, receiving cryptic images and whispers of the area's history; interpretation may require a Cohesion test (GM's DC).

talents.name: True Stoneshaping
talents.summary: Shape infused stone intricately and extensively without spending additional actions or time.

talents.name: Flowing Earth
talents.summary: After using Cohesion or spending at least 1 action on its talents, the stone underfoot pushes you up to 5 feet × your Cohesion ranks in any direction (including upward on a pillar) without extra Investiture; this movement doesn't trigger Reactive Strikes.

talents.name: Through the Stone
talents.summary: Use your surges and their talents as though your reach were 20 feet.

talents.name: Unbound Cohesion
talents.summary: Use Cohesion on any solid material that isn't alive, Invested, or infused with Stormlight — not just stone.

surges.name: Division
surges.summary: Decay and destruction. Base surge: touch a target (free hand) within reach. Vs. a character: spend 1 Investiture for a melee Division attack vs. Spiritual defense, 3d4 spirit damage (die grows with ranks); reducing them to 0 health crumbles them to dust. Vs. an object/area: spend Investiture equal to the surge-size cost and decay it as you choose (crumble, etch, smoke); in combat or other tense situations you must first succeed a Division test against a material-based DC (clear air 0, vapor 5, liquid 10, organics 15, solids 20). Can't target Invested/infused objects; unwilling characters' possessions/support surfaces need a Division test vs. their Physical defense or the material DC, whichever is higher.

talents.name: Bodily Decay
talents.summary: When you hit with a Division attack, spend an Opportunity to inflict an injury on one target of that attack.

talents.name: Igniting Division
talents.summary: When you affect a target with Division, spend 1+ additional Investiture to set them alight for that many rounds (testing Division under pressure first, if not already required): they become Afflicted, and the area within 5 feet is lit and becomes dangerous terrain, both dealing energy damage equal to your Division modifier. The target or an adjacent character may end the Afflicted early with an opposed Agility/Athletics test (the fire keeps burning regardless).

talents.name: Gout of Flame
talents.summary: Spend 3 Investiture; choose an area one size larger than your Division normally affects (25 feet at 5 ranks). Division attack vs. Physical defense of each target in the area, rolling 3d4 energy damage (die grows with ranks).

talents.name: Devastating Division
talents.summary: When you roll damage for Division or its talents, roll an additional damage die of the same size.

talents.name: Eroding Escape
talents.summary: Spend 1+ Investiture to end that many effects on yourself or a reachable ally, where each ended effect is either the Immobilized/Restrained condition or a disadvantage on physical tests; the underlying source may reassert itself later at the GM's discretion.

talents.name: Spark Sending
talents.summary: Use Division and its talents as though your reach were 20 feet, as long as a solid surface connects you to the target.

talents.name: Inescapable Spark
talents.summary: Your Spark Sending reach extends to your spren bond range and no longer needs line of effect (just the ability to sense the target); targets also can't Brace against your Division attacks.

talents.name: Unleashed Entropy
talents.summary: Using the base Division skill (not its talents) costs 1 less action. Your Division Under Pressure DCs are reduced by 5.

surges.name: Gravitation
surges.summary: Basic Lashings — redirects gravity. Base surge: touch a target within reach (up to your surge size, free hand) and spend 1+ Investiture (1/round to maintain) to change its gravity direction using your gravitation rate (starts at 25 feet). A willing character gains a flying rate equal to that rate (or infuse yourself, any size); an object moves that distance each turn in a straight line. No fall damage on impact unless aloft when the surge ends. Can't target Invested/infused objects. Against an unwilling character: Gravitation test vs. Physical defense, then spend 1 Investiture to move them (they become Restrained until your next turn; they can grab a passed object one size larger via an opposed Avoid Danger/Agility test to stop). Tricky maneuvers (highstorms, tight gaps) need a Gravitation test at the GM's DC.

talents.name: Flying Ace
talents.summary: While maintaining a Basic Lashing on yourself, fly up to your gravitation rate; once during that movement, spend 1 focus to make a melee weapon attack mid-flight, then continue moving. Your gravitation rate permanently increases to 40 feet.

talents.name: Stable Flight
talents.summary: While maintaining a Basic Lashing on yourself, your ranged attacks don't gain a disadvantage from flying or unstable footing.

talents.name: Group Flight
talents.summary: Out of combat, when you spend Investiture to infuse yourself or a willing character with a Basic Lashing, also infuse up to your Gravitation ranks in additional willing characters within reach at no extra Investiture cost; their infusions last as long as the original.

talents.name: Aerial Squadron
talents.summary: You can use your Group Flight talent in combat.

talents.name: Gravitational Slam
talents.summary: When a Basic Lashing collides an unwilling target with a solid surface, the collision stops the movement early and you may deal 1d4 impact damage per 10 feet moved this turn (die grows with ranks); the target may halve it via Avoid Danger opposed by your Gravitation.

talents.name: Multiple Lashings
talents.summary: After succeeding a Gravitation test to move an unwilling character, you may infuse them with Investiture up to your Gravitation ranks (instead of just 1); the effect then lasts until that infusion runs out instead of only until your next turn.

talents.name: Lashing Shot
talents.summary: Touch an unattended object up to your surge size and propel it at a target in your line of effect, spending Investiture equal to the distance divided by your gravitation rate (rounded up). Ranged Gravitation attack vs. Physical defense, rolling 2d4 impact damage (die grows with ranks).

talents.name: Master of the Skies
talents.summary: While you have at least 1 Investiture, you gain the benefits of being infused with Gravitation without spending Investiture.

surges.name: Illumination
surges.summary: Lightweaving — light-and-sound illusions. Base surge: spend 1+ Investiture to infuse an illusion into thin air within your spren bond range (up to your surge size); simple/static illusions cost 1 Investiture/10 minutes, illusions of characters or complex objects cost 1/round. Or spend 1 Investiture to disguise yourself with no ongoing cost, ending it freely or when Investiture runs out. Simple illusions/disguises auto-convince unless a viewer has reason for suspicion; complex illusions may need an Illumination test vs. the viewer's Cognitive defense. You can control complex illusions within bond range (free action for mindless tasks, action for tasks needing attention); illusions have no senses of their own. A failed test or Complication lets a viewer notice artificiality without destroying the illusion; passersby can also test Deduction/Perception opposed by your Illumination to find its boundaries.

talents.name: Distracting Illusion
talents.summary: Spend 1 Investiture to Lightweave a moving illusory duplicate of yourself or a sensed ally in their space; attacks against that character gain a disadvantage and can't graze until an attack misses them or the scene ends.

talents.name: Disorienting Flash
talents.summary: Spend 1 Investiture to project a burst of light/sound in an area within reach (up to your surge size); one Illumination test vs. each character's Cognitive defense in the area, each success making that character Disoriented until the end of their next turn.

talents.name: Spiritual Illumination
talents.summary: Spend 2 Investiture to create a momentary Lightweaving near a sensed ally within bond range; they become Determined and Focused until the end of their next turn.

talents.name: Painful Truth
talents.summary: Spend 2 Investiture; Illumination test vs. Spiritual defense of a sensed target within bond range. Success: they're Slowed until the end of their next turn; on their next turn they must spend focus equal to your Illumination ranks (as a free action) or immediately Move as far from you as possible.

talents.name: Lingering Lightweaving
talents.summary: Instead of infusing an illusion into thin air, infuse its Investiture into a sphere or unencased gem within 5 feet; the illusion moves with that gem, and the infusion expends 1 Investiture per number of rounds equal to your Illumination ranks instead of every round.

talents.name: Stormlight Reclamation
talents.summary: After your infusions expend Investiture at the start of your turn, end any number of infusions within reach and recover the Investiture remaining in them.

talents.name: Multiplicative Lightweaving
talents.summary: When you Lightweave an illusion, create up to your Illumination ranks in additional illusions at no extra Investiture cost, lasting as long as the original.

talents.name: Endless Illusions
talents.summary: While you have at least 1 Investiture, your Illumination infusions within spren bond range expend no Investiture at the start of your turn.

surges.name: Progression
surges.summary: Growth (rapid plant growth) and Regrowth (healing) of living things. Base surge: touch a living target other than yourself within reach (free hand). Plant Growth: spend 1 Investiture to rapidly grow a living plant/seed to a chosen size (up to your surge size and the species' normal limits). Character Regrowth: infuse 1+ Investiture (1/round) into a living character; they recover 1d4 + your Progression modifier in health at the start of each of their turns (die grows with ranks). Regrowth's healing manifests according to the target's own self-image, not the caster's choice.

talents.name: Explosive Growth
talents.summary: Spend 1 Investiture to use Growth in an area up to your surge size; one Progression attack compared vs. the Physical defense of each character you choose there, rolling 2d4 impact or keen damage (die grows with ranks). Spend an Opportunity to Immobilize any number of hit targets until the end of your next turn.

talents.name: Overgrowth
talents.summary: When you infuse Growth into a plant, make a DC 15 Progression test as part of that action; success grows it beyond its species' limits (up to your surge size) and increases its current and total health by 2d4 (die grows with ranks).

talents.name: Reliable Progression
talents.summary: When a scaling die from Explosive Growth, Overgrowth, or Swift Regeneration (or a willing character's Regrowth healing roll) rolls below your Progression ranks, you may change it to equal your ranks instead (no action required).

talents.name: Injury Regrowth
talents.summary: Spend 2 Investiture to cause yourself or a willing touched character to instantly recover from a temporary injury of their choice, or spend 3 Investiture for a permanent injury.

talents.name: Swift Regeneration
talents.summary: When you use Regenerate, recover 1d6 + your Progression modifier in health instead of the normal amount (die grows with ranks). While you have at least 1 Investiture, you and characters infused with your Regrowth add your Progression modifier to injury rolls.

talents.name: Extended Regrowth
talents.summary: Regrowth infusions expend 1 Investiture per number of rounds equal to your Progression ranks, instead of every round.

talents.name: From the Brink
talents.summary: Spend 3 Investiture and touch the body of a willing character who died within the last minute to restore them to life.

talents.name: Font of Life
talents.summary: Using Progression and its talents costs 1 less action.
